title: teucrium montanum subsp. helianthemoides
intro: Teucrium montanum subsp. helianthemoides, also known as alpine germander or mountain germander, is a subspecies of the perennial herb Teucrium montanum. It is a plant native to the Mediterranean region, used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties. The plant's leaves and stems contain compounds with anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which may help alleviate digestive issues, reduce fever, and soothe skin irritations. Teucrium montanum subsp. helianthemoides can be prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in traditional cooking and as a flavoring agent in some recipes.
